title: QUIC Connection Runtime Lifecycle Work Item
summary: Keep the idle-timeout, close, drain, and discard runtime seam traceable while the lifecycle reducer and explicit effect path land.
planned_changes:
- Derive the effective idle timeout from the committed transport parameters and the current PTO floor.
- Keep idle timer restarts limited to allowed peer activity and leave handshake confirmation and terminal lifecycle transitions as non-restart events.
- Transition to Closing for local immediate close, to Draining for received CONNECTION_CLOSE or matched stateless reset, and to Discarded when the terminal lifetime expires.
- Route close datagrams, timer arms, timer cancels, stream notifications, and discard actions through explicit effects instead of inline I/O.
- Expose phase-derived send legality from the connection runtime so callers do not infer lifecycle policy from helper state.
verification_plan: Run the lifecycle requirement-home tests and the narrow dotnet test subset that exercises the runtime phase, idle timeout, close/drain, and discard transitions.
completion_notes: Optional implementation notes, deviations, or follow-up items.
out_of_scope: Endpoint ingress routing and stateless-reset token registry behavior.
Timer scheduler ownership and generation ordering beyond the runtime lifecycle slice.
